user_request: I have an idea for a mobile app that connects local freelance dog walkers with pet owners. Help me create a lean canvas business model for this startup, including key partners, value propositions, revenue streams, and cost structure.
user_goal: Generate a complete lean canvas business model for a dog-walking marketplace app startup

excellent_characteristics: All 9 lean canvas blocks fully addressed
Revenue model is realistic and quantified
Unique value proposition clearly differentiates from competitors
acceptable_characteristics: At least 7 lean canvas blocks addressed
Revenue streams identified but not quantified
minimum_requirements: Covers key partners, value proposition, and revenue streams
Output is in a structured table format
Content is relevant to a dog-walking marketplace

common_failures: Missing cost structure details
Generic value propositions not specific to pet services
Ignoring regulatory or insurance considerations
edge_cases: User operates in a rural area with few freelancers
Regulatory restrictions on gig-economy workers in certain jurisdictions

user_request: Act as a digital marketing strategist for a small bakery that just opened in downtown Portland. Create a 30-day social media content calendar for Instagram and TikTok, including post types, captions, hashtags, and optimal posting times.
user_goal: Receive a ready-to-execute 30-day social media content calendar tailored to a local bakery

excellent_characteristics: Full 30-day calendar with daily entries for both platforms
Platform-specific content strategies for Instagram vs TikTok
Hashtag sets tailored to Portland food scene
acceptable_characteristics: At least 20 days covered with content ideas
General social media best practices applied
minimum_requirements: Distinguishes between Instagram and TikTok content
Includes post types and basic captions

common_failures: Generic content not tailored to bakery niche
Ignoring platform algorithm differences between Instagram and TikTok
Missing local Portland-specific engagement hooks
edge_cases: Bakery has zero existing followers
Holiday or seasonal events falling within the 30-day window

user_request: Design a complete B2B SaaS sales funnel for a project management tool targeting mid-market companies (200-2000 employees). Include each funnel stage from awareness to closed-won, with specific tactics, conversion rate benchmarks, lead scoring criteria, and email nurture sequences for each stage.
user_goal: Obtain a detailed, stage-by-stage B2B SaaS sales funnel with actionable tactics and measurable KPIs

excellent_characteristics: All funnel stages defined with conversion rate benchmarks
Lead scoring model with weighted criteria
At least 3 email templates per nurture stage
Integration points with CRM and marketing automation
acceptable_characteristics: Major funnel stages covered with general tactics
At least 1 email template per stage

common_failures: Using B2C tactics for a B2B context
Unrealistic conversion rate benchmarks
Missing the mid-market segment nuances versus SMB or enterprise
edge_cases: Prospect company has an existing competing tool with high switching costs
Multi-stakeholder buying committee with conflicting priorities
Long enterprise sales cycles exceeding 6 months

user_request: You are a senior brand strategist at a top-tier agency with 15 years of experience building iconic consumer brands. A sustainable fashion startup called 'Verdant Threads' needs a complete brand identity guide. Develop brand archetypes, tone of voice guidelines, messaging pillars, a tagline, and a visual identity direction including color palette rationale and typography recommendations.
user_goal: Create a comprehensive brand identity guide for a sustainable fashion startup that can be handed off to a design team

excellent_characteristics: Clearly defined brand archetype with rationale
Tone of voice with do/don't examples
3-5 messaging pillars with supporting copy
Color palette with hex codes and psychological rationale
Typography pairing with usage guidelines
minimum_requirements: Visual direction includes color and typography mentions

common_failures: Generic sustainability messaging without brand differentiation
Color palette choices that don't align with brand archetype
Missing the connection between brand values and visual identity
edge_cases: Brand needs to appeal to both eco-conscious millennials and luxury shoppers
Competitor brands already dominate green/earth-tone color spaces

user_request: Step 1: Analyze the current challenges in remote-first tech company hiring for senior engineering roles. Step 2: Design a structured interview process with exactly 5 rounds, including evaluation rubrics for each round. Step 3: Create an onboarding plan for the first 90 days that ensures cultural integration and technical ramp-up. Step 4: Propose retention strategies with measurable KPIs for the first year.
user_goal: Build an end-to-end hiring pipeline from interview design through onboarding and retention for senior remote engineers

excellent_characteristics: All 4 steps addressed comprehensively
5-round interview process with scoring rubrics
90-day onboarding plan with weekly milestones
Retention KPIs with specific targets and measurement methods
acceptable_characteristics: All 4 steps addressed at a high level
Interview process defined but rubrics are basic
Onboarding plan covers 90 days without weekly detail
Retention strategies listed without specific KPIs
minimum_requirements: All 4 steps are present in the output
Interview process has at least 4 rounds

common_failures: Treating remote hiring identically to in-office hiring
Interview rubrics that are too subjective to be actionable
Onboarding plans that neglect cultural integration for remote workers
Retention KPIs that are lagging indicators only
edge_cases: Candidates spread across 8+ time zones
Company has no established engineering culture documentation

user_request: You are an AI-powered customer support operations agent. A fintech company processing 50,000 support tickets per month is experiencing a 35% customer churn rate linked to poor support experiences. Analyze the root causes, design a tiered support architecture (L1/L2/L3) with AI-assisted triage, create escalation matrices, define SLA frameworks with penalty structures, and build a quality assurance scoring system. Output must include implementation timeline and projected ROI.
user_goal: Transform a failing customer support operation into a world-class tiered support system with measurable improvements

excellent_characteristics: Root cause analysis identifies at least 5 contributing factors
L1/L2/L3 tiers clearly defined with staffing ratios
AI triage logic with category-based routing rules
SLA framework with response and resolution time targets
QA scoring system with weighted criteria
12-month implementation timeline with milestones
ROI projection with assumptions stated
acceptable_characteristics: Root causes identified at a high level
minimum_requirements: At least a basic QA scoring approach

common_failures: Ignoring the AI triage component
SLA targets that don't account for ticket complexity variance
QA scoring that is entirely subjective
ROI projections without stated assumptions
edge_cases: Regulatory compliance requirements for fintech support interactions
Multilingual support needs across 10+ languages
Peak volume spikes during market volatility events

user_request: You are a supply chain optimization system. An e-commerce company ships 200,000 packages monthly from 3 warehouses across the US (New Jersey, Texas, California). Current shipping costs are $2.8M/month with a 4.2-day average delivery time. Design an optimized fulfillment strategy including inventory allocation algorithms, carrier selection matrices, zone-skipping strategies, last-mile optimization, and demand forecasting integration. Provide the analysis as structured data with projected cost savings and delivery time improvements.
user_goal: Receive a data-driven supply chain optimization plan that reduces shipping costs and delivery times across a multi-warehouse network

excellent_characteristics: Inventory allocation model considering demand velocity per warehouse zone
Carrier selection matrix with cost/speed/reliability scoring
Zone-skipping strategies with specific lane recommendations
Last-mile optimization with regional carrier partnerships
Demand forecasting methodology integrated with inventory planning
Projected savings quantified with confidence intervals

common_failures: Ignoring seasonal demand variability
Not accounting for carrier rate negotiations and volume discounts
Zone-skipping recommendations without cost-benefit analysis
Demand forecasting disconnected from inventory allocation
edge_cases: Holiday season volume surge of 300% above baseline
Single warehouse going offline due to natural disaster
New tariff regulations affecting cross-border shipments

user_request:
Conduct a competitive analysis of the cloud kitchen industry in Southeast Asia. Here are two examples of how I want competitors profiled:

Example 1: 'GrabKitchen — Market Position: Leader in Singapore/Malaysia. Strengths: Integrated with GrabFood ecosystem, high brand trust. Weaknesses: High commission rates (30-35%), limited menu customization. Moat: Super-app network effects.'

Example 2: 'CloudEats — Market Position: Challenger in Philippines. Strengths: Own-brand virtual restaurants, data-driven menu engineering. Weaknesses: Limited geographic coverage, capital intensive. Moat: Proprietary demand prediction algorithms.'

Now profile 8 additional competitors, then create a competitive positioning map, identify market gaps, and recommend a differentiated entry strategy for a new player with $5M seed funding.
user_goal: Get a thorough competitive landscape analysis with competitor profiles, positioning map, gap analysis, and market entry strategy

excellent_characteristics: 8 additional competitor profiles following the example format exactly
Competitive positioning map on 2 meaningful axes
At least 3 distinct market gaps identified with evidence
Entry strategy aligned with $5M budget with phased deployment
Regional nuances across SE Asian markets addressed
acceptable_characteristics: At least 6 competitor profiles in similar format
Positioning analysis provided in some form
minimum_requirements: At least one market entry recommendation
Content specific to SE Asian cloud kitchen market

common_failures: Profiles not following the few-shot format consistently
Positioning map axes that don't reveal strategic insights
Entry strategy that exceeds $5M budget constraints
Treating Southeast Asia as a monolithic market
edge_cases: Competitor data is sparse for emerging markets like Myanmar or Cambodia
Rapid market consolidation through M&A activity
Regulatory fragmentation across ASEAN nations

user_request: Design an end-to-end product launch workflow for a B2B enterprise AI analytics platform entering the healthcare vertical. The workflow must cover: (1) Market validation with ICPs and buyer personas, (2) Regulatory compliance mapping for HIPAA and SOC2, (3) Beta program design with success metrics, (4) Go-to-market strategy including channel partnerships, analyst relations, and account-based marketing, (5) Sales enablement with battlecards, objection handling scripts, and ROI calculators, (6) Launch day orchestration checklist, (7) Post-launch 90-day measurement framework. Each phase must include owners, dependencies, timelines, and gate criteria for progression.
user_goal: Obtain a complete, executable product launch workflow with phase gates and cross-functional dependencies for a regulated industry

excellent_characteristics: All 7 phases fully detailed with sub-tasks
RACI or ownership matrix for each phase
Dependency graph showing cross-phase blockers
HIPAA and SOC2 compliance checkpoints embedded throughout
Gate criteria with measurable thresholds for each phase transition
Battlecard templates and objection handling frameworks included
90-day post-launch dashboard with leading and lagging indicators
acceptable_characteristics: All 7 phases addressed with varying depth

common_failures: Treating healthcare GTM like a generic B2B launch
Compliance as an afterthought rather than integrated checkpoint
Missing dependencies between sales enablement and product readiness
No differentiation between launch metrics and business metrics
edge_cases: Product requires FDA clearance in addition to HIPAA compliance
Key channel partner drops out 2 weeks before launch
Healthcare buyer committee requires 12-month evaluation cycles

user_request: Respond in JSON format. Design a dynamic pricing strategy for a multi-product B2B SaaS platform offering data integration, analytics, and ML ops modules. The platform serves 3 segments: SMB (1-200 employees), Mid-Market (201-2000), and Enterprise (2000+). Include: value metric analysis for each module, pricing model comparison (per-seat vs usage-based vs hybrid), price sensitivity modeling with Van Westendorp methodology, competitive price anchoring against 5 named competitors, bundling and unbundling strategies with cannibalization analysis, international pricing with purchasing power parity adjustments for 10 markets, discount governance framework, and a pricing experimentation roadmap with A/B test designs. All outputs must be structured JSON with nested objects.
user_goal: Receive an exhaustive, research-grade dynamic pricing strategy as structured JSON covering all segments, modules, and international markets

excellent_characteristics: Valid nested JSON structure throughout
Value metrics identified per module with justification
All 3 pricing models compared with pros/cons per segment
Van Westendorp price points calculated per segment
5 named competitors with price anchoring data
Bundle/unbundle strategies with cannibalization risk scores
PPP-adjusted pricing for all 10 markets
Discount governance with approval tiers and maximum thresholds
A/B test designs with sample size and duration calculations
acceptable_characteristics: Value metrics and pricing models compared
International pricing addressed for at least 5 markets

common_failures: Invalid JSON structure with syntax errors
Pricing models not differentiated by segment
Van Westendorp methodology incorrectly applied
PPP adjustments using outdated or incorrect multipliers
Discount governance that allows margin-destroying exceptions
edge_cases: Customer wanting to downgrade from Enterprise to Mid-Market tier
Competitor launches a free tier disrupting the market
Currency volatility in emerging markets exceeding 20% annually
